headline: John Wick's 'Ballerina' Rebounds: Streaming Salvation for Franchise's Dark Horse
lede: After a rocky theatrical run, Ana de Armas' 'Ballerina' is staging a dramatic comeback on streaming platforms, breathing new life into the Wick universe.
body:
The John Wick franchise's peripheral spinoff 'Ballerina' is rewriting its own narrative one streaming click at a time. Initially considered a commercial disappointment, the Keanu Reeves-adjacent action thriller starring Ana de Armas has found unexpected redemption through digital distribution.

Indication of the film's streaming renaissance emerged this week, with digital platform metrics suggesting robust viewer engagement. The movie, which centers on a vengeful ballerina trained as an assassin, has rocketed up streaming charts despite its modest box office performance.

Industry analysts attribute the turnaround to several key factors. De Armas' rising star power, combined with the film's stylized action choreography and direct narrative connections to the Wick mythology, have proven compelling for home viewers. The streaming rebound also underscores a growing trend of franchise extensions finding second lives beyond traditional theatrical windows.

Lionsgate, the studio behind the John Wick franchise, appears cautiously optimistic about 'Ballerina's trajectory. The film's digital performance could potentially influence future spinoff strategies for the action franchise, which has consistently demonstrated resilience and audience loyalty.
key_facts: Streaming metrics show significant viewer engagement
Part of John Wick expanded universe
